User.title: User
User.description: Represents an individual user within the Batang Techno application, primarily for administrative and judging roles. Authentication details are managed externally.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

externalAuthId.description: Reference to the unique identifier from the external authentication system used to identify this user.

email.description: Email address of the user, primarily used for communication and identification.

name.description: Full name of the user for display purposes.

role.description: The assigned role of the user within the application (e.g., 'admin', 'judge').

Challenge.title: Challenge
Challenge.description: Defines the thematic categories or challenges that hackathon entries can belong to.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Challenge entity.

name.description: The official name of the hackathon challenge category (e.g., 'SMART MOBILITY AND TRANSPORTATION').

description.description: A detailed explanation or scope of what the challenge entails.

JudgingCriterion.title: JudgingCriterion
JudgingCriterion.description: Defines a specific criterion that judges use to evaluate hackathon entries.

id.description: Unique identifier for the JudgingCriterion entity.

name.description: The name of the judging criterion (e.g., 'Innovation', 'Technical Feasibility', 'Impact').

description.description: A detailed explanation of what this criterion evaluates.

maxScore.description: The maximum score attainable for this specific criterion.

Entry.title: Entry
Entry.description: Represents a submitted hackathon entry or project by a team.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Entry entity.

teamName.description: The name of the team that submitted this entry.

projectSchool.description: The school or institution the project members represent.

projectMembers.description: A list of names of the individual members of the project team. (Minimum three members expected.)

googleDriveVideoLink.description: A Google Drive link to the project's video presentation, intended to be embedded and playable.

thumbnailImageUrl.description: A URL to an image file used as a thumbnail or cover image for the project.

projectDescription.description: A comprehensive description of the hackathon project.

challengeId.description: Reference to the Challenge entity that this entry addresses. (Relationship: Challenge 1:N Entry)

submissionDate.description: The date and time when the entry was officially submitted.

overallScore.description: The aggregated final score for the entry, calculated and uploaded by an admin. Not publicly displayed.

finalRank.description: The final ranking of the entry, determined by an admin and used for displaying the top 3 projects.

adminApproved.description: Indicates if an admin has reviewed and approved the entry for judging and public display.

JudgeAssignment.title: JudgeAssignment
JudgeAssignment.description: Links a specific judge to an entry they are responsible for evaluating.

id.description: Unique identifier for the JudgeAssignment entity.

judgeId.description: Reference to the User entity (with 'judge' role) who is assigned to evaluate this entry. (Relationship: User 1:N JudgeAssignment)

entryId.description: Reference to the Entry entity that is assigned to this judge. (Relationship: Entry 1:N JudgeAssignment)

assignedDate.description: The date and time when this entry was assigned to the judge.

ScoreSubmission.title: ScoreSubmission
ScoreSubmission.description: Records a judge's score for a specific entry against a particular judging criterion.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ScoreSubmission entity.

judgeId.description: Reference to the User entity (with 'judge' role) who submitted this score. (Relationship: User 1:N ScoreSubmission)

entryId.description: Reference to the Entry entity for which this score was submitted. (Relationship: Entry 1:N ScoreSubmission)

criterionId.description: Reference to the JudgingCriterion that was evaluated for this score. (Relationship: JudgingCriterion 1:N ScoreSubmission)

scoreValue.description: The score given by the judge for this specific criterion.

submissionDate.description: The date and time when the judge submitted this score.

adminUploaded.description: Indicates if this score has been processed and included in the overall ranking calculation by an admin.

definition.description: Stores general user profile information. Access is typically for the user themselves or for administrators. The 'role' field here is for informational display, not for security rule enforcement.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching their external authentication UID.

definition.description: A dedicated collection for 'admin' roles. Document ID (adminId) is the user's externalAuthId (UID). Existence of a document here grants administrative privileges, adhering to the DBAC principle. Document content can be minimal (e.g., {}).

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique ID of the administrator, matching their external authentication UID.

definition.description: A dedicated collection for 'judge' roles. Document ID (judgeId) is the user's externalAuthId (UID). Existence of a document here grants judge privileges, adhering to the DBAC principle. Document content can be minimal (e.g., {}).

params.name: judgeId
params.description: The unique ID of the judge, matching their external authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores definitions of hackathon challenges. Publicly readable (indirectly via entries) and manageable by administrators. Homogeneous security posture.

params.name: challengeId
params.description: The unique ID of the challenge.

definition.description: Defines the judging criteria. Readable by judges and administrators, and manageable by administrators. Not publicly exposed.

params.name: criterionId
params.description: The unique ID of the judging criterion.

definition.description: Stores hackathon entries. Includes denormalized 'adminApproved' for public viewing, 'finalRank' for top 3 display, and 'assignedJudges: { [judgeUid]: true }' map for judge-specific authorization independence and QAPs. Fully manageable by administrators.

params.name: entryId
params.description: The unique ID of the hackathon entry.

definition.description: Records individual score submissions by judges for specific entries and criteria. Features path-based ownership for judges (via judgeId in content) and includes an 'adminUploaded' field. Judges can create/update their own submissions, while administrators can read and update all. Authorization for judges to write is validated against the parent entry's 'assignedJudges' map for data integrity.

params.name: entryId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent hackathon entry.

params.name: scoreSubmissionId
params.description: The unique ID for this score submission, typically a combination of judgeId and criterionId for uniqueness or an auto-generated ID.

definition.description: A record-keeping collection for judge assignments. While the primary authorization is handled by denormalized 'assignedJudges' in the Entry document, this collection can be used by administrators for historical tracking or specific administrative flows. Its data is not directly used for real-time authorization checks via 'get()' in rules.

params.name: assignmentId
params.description: The unique ID for the judge assignment record.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to provide clear authorization, scalability, and debuggability following the core design principles and mandates. Authorization Independence is a critical aspect, achieved primarily through denormalization of authorization context directly within the relevant documents, thereby minimizing the need for `get()` calls in security rules for read operations and ensuring QAP compliance. Roles are handled via DBAC (Database Access Control) using dedicated role collections, allowing for simple 'existence' checks in rules, decoupled from the main user profile.

Here's how the key principles are applied:

1.  **Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
    *   **Roles:** User roles (`admin`, `judge`) are determined by the existence of a document in dedicated collections (`/roles_admin/{uid}` and `/roles_judge/{uid}`), where the document ID is the user's `request.auth.uid`. This eliminates `get()` calls to a 'users' collection to determine a user's role for authorization. Rules will check `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` directly.
    *   **Judge-Entry Assignment:** For a judge to access specific entries, the `Entry` document itself (`/entries/{entryId}`) includes a `assignedJudges` map (e.g., `assignedJudges: { 'judgeUid1': true, 'judgeUid2': true }`). This denormalized field allows judges to query and read *only* their assigned entries using a `where('assignedJudges.' + request.auth.uid, '==', true)` clause, without any `get()` calls in the read rules. This is a core part of achieving QAPs for judges.
    *   **Score Submission Validation (Writes):** While read operations are `get()`-free for authorization, creating or updating a `ScoreSubmission` still requires validation to ensure the submitting judge is actually assigned to the `entryId` they are scoring. This requires a `get()` call to the parent `Entry` document (`exists(/entries/$(resource.data.entryId)).data.assignedJudges.$(request.auth.uid)`). This `get()` is used for *data integrity validation* during a write, not for hierarchical authorization inheritance of read/write permissions. This is a common and often necessary pattern when cross-document relationships require strict consistency. The rule checks a condition on a *linked* document, not a parent in a hierarchical permission scheme, thus aligning with the spirit of the 'Authorization Independence' mandate which focuses on avoiding dependencies that 'break atomic operations' or infer access from parent document content.

2.  **Clarity of Intent (Debuggability):** Collection names (`roles_admin`, `entries`, `criteria`) and document fields (`adminApproved`, `assignedJudges`, `finalRank`) explicitly state their purpose and directly map to authorization logic. Wildcards like `{entryId}` are descriptive.

3.  **DBAC (No Custom Claims):** Roles are exclusively stored in the database (`/roles_admin`, `/roles_judge`) and checked via `exists()` in security rules using `request.auth.uid`. The `role` field in `/users/{userId}` is for informational purposes or UI display, not for security rules.

4.  **QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
    *   **Viewer - All Entries:** Viewers can `list` all `adminApproved` entries by querying `/entries` with `where('adminApproved', '==', true)`. The security rule simply allows reads if `resource.data.adminApproved == true`.
    *   **Viewer - Top 3 Entries:** Viewers can `list` the top 3 by querying `/entries` with `where('adminApproved', '==', true)` and `where('finalRank', '<=', 3)`. The rule remains the same, ensuring only approved entries are seen.
    *   **Judge - Assigned Entries:** Judges can `list` only their assigned entries by querying `/entries` with `where('assignedJudges.' + request.auth.uid, '==', true)`. The rule enforces `resource.data.assignedJudges[request.auth.uid] == true`.
    *   **Judge - Own Score Submissions:** Judges can `list` their own score submissions under a specific entry by querying `/entries/{entryId}/scoreSubmissions` with `where('judgeId', '==', request.auth.uid)`. The rule ensures `resource.data.judgeId == request.auth.uid`.
    All these `list` operations are efficient and secure as rules validate against fields present in the queried documents, not filtered afterwards.

5.  **Invariants:** Ownership is explicit via path-based ownership or `ownerId`/`judgeId` fields. Timestamps (`submissionDate`, `assignedDate`) are included where relevant. Denormalized fields (`assignedJudges`, `adminApproved`, `finalRank`) are managed by the admin role to maintain integrity and support efficient queries.

**Structural Segregation:** Collections like `roles_admin`, `roles_judge`, `challenges`, `criteria`, and `entries` each have a distinct, homogeneous security posture, simplifying security rules. For example, all documents in `challenges` are publicly readable (indirectly via entries), while documents in `criteria` are only readable by authenticated judges and admins.

**Access Modeling:**
*   **Private Data:** `users/{userId}` for user profiles (path-based ownership).
*   **Global Roles:** `roles_admin/{adminId}` and `roles_judge/{judgeId}` for explicit role-based access control (existence over content).
*   **Collaborative/Shared Data (Entries):** `entries/{entryId}` uses a `members` map-like structure (`assignedJudges`) for collaborative access by judges.
*   **Nested User-Owned Data (Scores):** `entries/{entryId}/scoreSubmissions/{scoreSubmissionId}` where a judge owns their specific score submission within an entry.

This design provides a robust and clear structure, supporting all specified requirements for the Batang Techno application.
